title: narcissus patulus subsp. etruscus
intro: Narcissus patulus subsp. etruscus, commonly known as the Etruscan daffodil, is a subspecies of the narcissus plant that is native to Europe and the Mediterranean region. The Etruscan daffodil has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which have been attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. Etruscan daffodil is often used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and ointments, and is typically harvested in the summer months when its flowers are in full bloom.
